summary:
Abdominal CT is not universally required in intoxicated blunt trauma patients; it should only be performed when there are specific clinical indicators such as symptoms or abnormal labs suggesting intra-abdominal injury. 

\boxed{\text{No}} The necessity of an abdominal CT scan after blunt trauma in intoxicated individuals depends upon presenting clinical findings and laboratory results rather than intoxication status alone. This indicates that routine imaging isn't justified without clear indication. Therefore, the correct response is no—computed tomography of the abdomen is not always necessary. Final Answer:\boxed{\text{No}}
